POINTS:
- [transports/sse/deployment/src/test/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/test/McpServerTest.java:47] @mvduijnhoven shoudn't we add `quarkus.mcp.server.schema-generator.swagger2.enabled=false` as well?
v8_review:
SUMMARY:
This PR adds support for customizing the Victools SchemaGenerator via a CDI-based `SchemaGeneratorConfigCustomizer` interface, with built-in customizers for Jackson, Jakarta Validation, and Swagger2 modules. The approach of auto-detecting optional dependencies at build time and registering corresponding customizer beans is clean and follows Quarkus conventions. Key concerns include a missing ordering convention in the new interface, potential native-image reflection gaps, and documentation formatting issues.

POINTS:
- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/runtime/SchemaGeneratorConfigCustomizer.java:1-20] The new `SchemaGeneratorConfigCustomizer` interface is missing documentation about priority-based ordering. All other similar `@All List<...>` interfaces in this codebase (`ToolFilter`, `PromptFilter`, `ResourceFilter`, `ResourceTemplateFilter`, `InitialCheck`) document that "Multiple [items] are sorted by `InjectableBean#getPriority()` and executed sequentially. Higher priority is executed first." Since customizers modify the same `SchemaGeneratorConfigBuilder` instance and execution order matters when multiple customizers configure the same options, add this documentation for consistency and to inform users who write custom customizers.

- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/runtime/SchemaGeneratorConfigCustomizerJackson.java:44] The `getConfiguredOptions()` method enables several options by default (`respectJsonPropertyOrder`, `respectJsonPropertyRequired`, `flattenedEnumsFromJsonValue` are all `@WithDefault("true")`). Consider whether these defaults are appropriate for all users — enabling `RESPECT_JSONPROPERTY_REQUIRED` and `RESPECT_JSONPROPERTY_ORDER` by default changes schema generation behavior compared to the previous baseline (no Jackson module). This is a behavioral change for users who add the Jackson dependency but may not expect these options enabled.

- [core/deployment/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/deployment/JsonSchemaGeneratorProcessor.java:34-43] The `JsonSchemaGeneratorProcessor` does not register the victools module classes (`JacksonModule`, `JakartaValidationModule`, `Swagger2Module`, and their corresponding `*Option` enums) for reflection. These classes are instantiated via `new JacksonModule(options)`, etc. at runtime in the customizer classes. While the base code's use of `SchemaGenerator` and `SchemaGeneratorConfigBuilder` also lacks reflection registration (a pre-existing concern), this PR extends the surface area. Consider whether `ReflectiveClassBuildItem` registration is needed for native-image compatibility when these optional modules are present.

- [docs/modules/ROOT/pages/index.adoc:568-569] Two documentation issues: (1) "the schema generator server will be automatically configured" contains an extra word — should be "the schema generator will be automatically configured". (2) Missing closing backtick: the text reads `` `com.github.victools:jsonschema-module-jakarta-validation and `com.github.victools:jsonschema-module-swagger-2` `` but should be `` `com.github.victools:jsonschema-module-jakarta-validation` and `com.github.victools:jsonschema-module-swagger-2` ``.

- [transports/sse/deployment/src/test/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/test/McpServerTest.java:46-48] Good practice: the base test class disables all three schema generator modules by default in `defaultConfig()`, ensuring existing tests are not affected by the new optional dependencies added to the test classpath. This prevents test flakiness and maintains backward compatibility in the test suite.

- [core/runtime/src/main/java/io/quarkiverse/mcp/server/runtime/ToolManagerImpl.java:244-252] The `constructSchemaGenerator` method applies customizers in CDI injection order (via `@All List<...>`). While CDI respects `@Priority` for ordering, there's no explicit `Prioritized` extension on the interface. This is consistent with the existing `ToolFilter` pattern in the codebase, but the lack of documented ordering contract (see point 1) makes it unclear whether users should rely on priority annotations to control customizer sequencing.
